things.name: Guinness Storehouse
things.description: Ireland's most popular attraction — seven floors of brewing history with a panoramic Gravity Bar pint

things.name: Trinity College & Book of Kells
things.description: Ireland's oldest university (1592) housing the illuminated 9th-century Book of Kells manuscript

things.name: Dublin Castle
things.description: Former seat of British power in Ireland with state apartments, a Viking undercroft and Georgian gardens

things.name: Temple Bar Quarter
things.description: Dublin's cultural quarter on the south bank of the Liffey — cobbled streets, pubs and live trad music

things.name: National Museum of Ireland
things.description: Archaeology branch with the Ardagh Chalice, Tara Brooch and famous bog bodies

things.name: Phoenix Park
things.description: One of Europe's largest enclosed public parks at 1,750 acres — home to wild deer and the US Ambassador's residence

things.name: Kilmainham Gaol
things.description: Iconic former prison where leaders of the 1916 Easter Rising were executed — deeply moving tours

things.name: National Gallery of Ireland
things.description: Outstanding collection of Irish and European art including a dedicated Caravaggio

things.name: Grafton Street & St Stephen's Green
things.description: Dublin's premier pedestrian shopping street leading to the beautiful Victorian city park

things.name: Howth Head & DART Coastal Walk
things.description: Take the DART train to Howth for cliff walks, a fishing village, seals and excellent seafood
